messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**
Seems Psalms still speaks of enemies, but Chris the abolition of the enmity.

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

You have touched upon a profound distinction, one that separates the cOLD covenant of law from the new covenant of grace. Indeed, the Psalms are replete with imprecatory cries against enemies, for they operate under the ministration of condemnation and the law of sin and death. David could cry out for the destruction of his foes because he was under a system of earthly judgment and temporal consequence. But the Apostle Paul reveals a glorious mystery: Christ is our peace, who hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us, having abolished in his flesh the enmity, even the law of commandments contained in ordinances (Eph 2:14-15). The law created enmity; Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone that believeth (Rom 10:4). Why? Because the law was a ministry of death, written and engraven in stones (2 Cor 3:7), but the Spirit gives life.

The key is to see the "2Sons" Jesus: the Son of man, who came to judge and bring a sword (Mt 10:34), and the Son of God, who came not to condemn the world, but that the world through him might be saved (Jn 3:17). The Psalms speak to the Son of man ministry, where enemies are real and judgment is imminent. But in Christ, the "amnos" Lamb of God, the sin of the world is taken away (Jn 1:29). There is no enmity left for the believer who is in Christ, for God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them (2 Cor 5:19). The "arnion" Lamb of Revelation is a different matter, dealing with nations and the unrepentant at the end of the age. But for the believer, the old man is crucified with him, that the body of sin might be destroyed (Rom 6:6). How can there be enmity when the cause of enmity—the law in our members—has been rendered null and void through the cross? [^1]

This is why we must learn the Lamb vs Lamb difference. The Son of God is the Lamb that taketh away; the Son of man is the Lamb that smites. The Psalms cry out for the smiting, but the gospel announces the taking away. The believer is not "under" the government of the Psalms' imprecations; they are "with" Christ, seated in heavenly places (Eph 2:6). The enmity is not just covered; it is abolished. It is not just forgiven; it is removed as far as the east is from the west (Ps 103:12). To try and mix the two is to create a "LukeWarm" gospel that neither judges righteously nor saves completely. The grace of God teaches us that we have no enemy in the flesh, for we wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against spiritual wickedness in high places (Eph 6:12). And even that spiritual enemy has been disarmed by the cross (Col 2:15). The Psalms are a historical record of a people under law; our standing is in Christ, where there is neither Jew nor Greek, bond nor free, but Christ is all, and in all (Col 3:11). [^2]

Learn More:
- [Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come](/pages/would_any_want_one_like_unto_the_son_of_man_to_come)
- [How much of the Bible is ALLEGORY 🤔Allegoric Mystery❓](/pages/how_much_of_the_bible_is_allegory_allegoric_mystery)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

1.title: Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come
1.snippet: God did NOT send Sons; And God sent NOT his Son to condemn. Perhaps that's why 'peace' & 'victory' are 'through Jesus→Christ'.

2.title: How much of the Bible is ALLEGORY 🤔Allegoric Mystery❓
2.snippet: Perhaps to allegory denote we need to get the correct God and correct Father to then have the correct Christ as 1 Son, not still twain (Son of man & Son of God) like Jesus first portrayed in Mt to Acts, and noted in John 5:25-29 it was 2 voices saying 2 things: 'live' & 'judgment also', as Son of God voice & Son of man voice.
